Multi-language TOTP (2FA) code manager that auto-fills one-time codes on verification forms.

English

Auto OTP Authenticator is a lightweight, privacy-focused TOTP (Time-based One-Time Password) manager for your browser. It generates one-time codes, helps you organize accounts, and can fill them into common 2FA / verification forms—so you don’t have to reach for your phone for every login.

After setup, TOTP codes are computed entirely on your device using the Web Crypto API. Secrets stay in your browser profile by default. There are no analytics servers, no telemetry, and no tracking from this extension.

What it supports

TOTP codes (RFC 6238) — typically 6 or 8 digits, 30-second (or custom) steps, HMAC-SHA1 or HMAC-SHA-256
Automatic detection & fill — supports single-field and multi-box OTP layouts
Domain-aware accounts — matches and prioritizes accounts per website
Popup workflow — search, filter, copy, delete, last-used priority
Keyboard shortcuts — copy/fill without opening popup
QR & otpauth — scan from page (including iframes) or import from image
Import & export — JSON backup/restore
Appearance — light/dark/system theme
Optional Chrome sync — stores data in Google sync (optional)
Languages — English, Turkish, German, Spanish, Italian

Privacy & security

Default: chrome.storage.local
Codes generated locally
No backend servers
Export backups manually
Not a replacement for hardware keys (e.g., YubiKey)



Turkish (Türkçe)

Auto OTP Authenticator, tarayıcınızda kullanabileceğiniz hafif ve gizlilik odaklı bir TOTP (zaman tabanlı tek kullanımlık şifre) yöneticisidir. Kod üretir, hesapları organize eder ve 2FA doğrulama alanlarına otomatik doldurur.

Kurulumdan sonra kodlar yalnızca cihazınızda Web Crypto API ile hesaplanır. Secret bilgiler tarayıcı profilinizde kalır. Analiz, takip veya telemetri yoktur.

Neleri destekler?

TOTP (RFC 6238) — 6/8 hane, 30 sn step, SHA1 / SHA256
Otomatik doldurma — tek alan & multi-box OTP
Domain bazlı hesap eşleşmesi
Popup üzerinden arama / filtre / kopyalama
Kısayollar — popup açmadan işlem
QR / otpauth tarama (iframe dahil)
JSON export/import
Tema seçenekleri
Chrome sync (opsiyonel)
Çok dil desteği

Gizlilik ve güvenlik

Depolama: chrome.storage.local
Kodlar local üretilir
Backend yok

Watch and Learn: Study a language while watching Netflix
Learn a language while enjoying your favourite shows on Netflix.

This tool helps you use Netflix to study English, or Spanish, or French or any other language.

Steps to get started:

1. Once installed, open up your favourite foreign-language show on Netflix.
2. When the show starts, you should see a sidebar appear on the right (if not, refresh the page).
3. Click the switch to “on” to enable the extension
4. The sidebar will load a vocabulary preview - a list of words and phrases that will be useful to know as you watch.
5. Once you have finished previewing vocabulary, you can hide the sidebar by clicking chevron (») at the top.
6. As the show plays, you can hover your mouse over any subtitle and the show will pause while you choose which word or phrase you need explaining. You get definitions, examples, a pronunciation guide and questions to check your understanding.
7. Most important step: give me your feedback, please! I want this tool to be useful. And I can only do that if I know where it needs to improve. Thanks!

TIP: The extension adds its own subtitles so if you see two sets of subtitles, then turn off the standard Netflix ones.


FAQ
------

*Question*
Do you have Finnish? Swedish? German? Swahili? etc etc etc

*Answer*
The extension should work with all of the most popular languages. If you find that your desired language is not working correctly, then let us know and we will look into adding it!

And if you are looking for Finnish, here is a screen recording of that language in action: https://res.cloudinary.com/dzyczavob/video/upload/v1724681773/samples/Screen_Recording_2024-08-26_at_15.12.59.mp4

*Question*
I can see two sets of subtitles and my eyes are hurting.

*Answer*
Ok, that isn't a question but I get what you mean. Make sure you have the original Netflix subtitles turned off. The extension will add an overlay of clickable subtitles and if you have the original subtitles showing as well then it can get confusing.

*Question*
But... Netflix already has subtitles in a variety of languages. Also, why would I watch Netflix in Chrome when I've got a big screen? When I watch series and films in languages other than English I like to set the audio to French and then use the subtitles to help me with whatever I can't catch from the audio. I don't really see what this extension would bring to the table?

*Answer*
The extension aims to augment the subtitle experience with definitions, explanations, and exercises to help you learn while watching. Maybe you'd find the dual subtitle feature useful? For example, here's a screen recording of Lupin with dual subtitles:

https://res.cloudinary.com/dzyczavob/video/upload/v1724706492/samples/Screen_Recording_2024-08-26_at_22.05.55.mp4

There is a trade off between watching video to learn a language and watching for entertainment. This extension hopes to reduce the friction if you want to access a detailed language reference while simultaneously enjoying the show. You can cable your laptop up to a big screen if you want more of a cinema experience :) but we can't get away from needing a browser extension to inject these features into the Netflix platform.

In the backlog there is a task to investigate Kodi, Jellyfin and other open source TV operating systems to try and bring these features to the bigger screen but for now we need to focus on getting the simpler stuff right.

ASIN Finder chrome extension finds and displays codes for all Amazon™ products with a single click.

ASIN Finder for Amazon is a powerful chrome extension designed to simplify your Amazon shopping experience. It helps you quickly locate and display ASIN (Amazon Standard Identification Number) codes for products listed on Amazon. ASIN codes are essential for various purposes, including tracking products and conducting research. 

 
Key Features: 

>> Seamless Integration: ASIN Finder for Chrome seamlessly integrates into your Chrome browser, making it easy to access ASIN codes without leaving the Amazon product page. 

>> One-Click ASIN Retrieval: With a single click, the extension automatically identifies and retrieves the ASIN code of the product you're viewing on Amazon. 

>> Multiple ASIN Lookup: Users can check multiple product ASIN at once on Amazon product search page, and it will fetch ASIN codes for all of them at once.   

How to Use ASIN Finder for Chrome?  

>> Click on the extension and click "Add to Chrome" to install it. 

>> Once installed, the extension icon will appear in your Chrome toolbar. 

>> Navigate to an Amazon product page you want to retrieve the ASIN code for. 

>> Click on the ASIN Finder icon in your Chrome toolbar  

  >> Now the extension will display the ASIN codes & users can copy or export them on the system  

  
ASIN Finder for Chrome streamlines your Amazon shopping experience by providing quick access to ASIN codes, ultimately saving you time and effort when researching or managing Amazon products.

The Ultimate Web Table Extraction Tool – Compatible with 99% of Websites and UI Frameworks!

The Ultimate Web Table Extraction Tool – Compatible with 99% of Websites and UI Frameworks!

Effortlessly extract tables from any website and take control of your data with our powerful web table extraction software. Perfect for developers, analysts, and professionals alike, this tool ensures seamless compatibility with 99% of websites and front-end UI frameworks. Say goodbye to manual table extraction and streamline your workflow with one-click simplicity.

Key Features:

- One-click Extraction of All Tables: Extract every table on a web page instantly with a single click, saving you valuable time.
- Unmatched Compatibility: Easily extract tables from 99% of websites and front-end frameworks, even those with complex layouts and dynamic forms.
- Export to Google Sheets: Directly export your extracted tables to Google Sheets for quick and easy collaboration.
- Batch Export to Clipboard, Excel, and Google Sheets: Extract and export multiple tables at once, reducing repetitive tasks.
- Capture Multi-page and Scrolling Tables: Automatically extract tables that span multiple pages or load dynamically as you scroll.
- Copy to Clipboard as Markdown: Easily copy tables in Markdown format for use in code documentation or text editors.
- Screenshot Tables (PNG Format): Instantly capture and save tables as high-quality PNG images, ideal for reports and presentations.
- Export to Office 365: Seamlessly transfer tables to Office 365 for editing, analysis, and presentation.
- Flexible Export Options: Download your tables in Excel, CSV, or HTML format, giving you maximum flexibility for data management.
- Customizable Table Selection: Fine-tune the selection of tables and data ranges to extract exactly what you need.
- Excel-ready Adaptations: Automatically adapt tables for easy use in Excel, eliminating formatting issues and simplifying data manipulation.
- Handles Complex Table Structures: Capable of extracting even the most intricate table layouts and structures, making sure no data is left behind.

Take the hassle out of data extraction and boost your productivity with our feature-rich table extraction tool. Whether it's for data analysis, reporting, or presentations, we’ve got you covered!

The extension allows users to manage a list of URLs of websites that are displayed in "inverted" mode (if the website has a dark…

The extension allows users to manage a list of URLs of websites that are displayed in "inverted" mode (if the website has a dark theme, it switches the website to light mode and viceversa). Users can add, view, update, and delete URLs from the list.

The extension has another handy feature: it allows users to count words or letters in the text selected on a website.

A Chrome extension to set the background of ANY page

A Chrome extension that can help you add background to almost any website you go.

1. Why using this extension:
---> If you tired of having boring background of websites and want to change it then this is a solver for you, with this extension you surf your favorite website with a background of your choice.

2. Benefits from this extension:
- Lightweight extension and optimized, not taking up your RAM and usage
- No ads and not sending user information to anywhere, everything is running under your local machine and google account (open-source soon?)
- Works with any other website manipulation extension
- Help you have a better focus on websites =w=

3. Features:
- Live Updates on websites whenever the settings changed
- Able to set the Z-index of the background to wherever you think it works for you
- Options to remember websites settings
- Can disable any time you want
- Dark mode for preview popup and options page or better preview since some websites are dark and others are light
- Works for local image too (this option actually help the extension to work anywhere since some website restricted to load image from links)
- Remember your settings across all your devices connected to your account (not the same case for the Local Image option, this require your account to turn on sync)
- Notice when the URL changed to activate the extension running, this will help on SPA websites
- Smooth User Interface for better experience

Disclaimer: image URL must be raw image, not the link of some image upload page, to get raw image right click into it and "open image in new tab" then copy that link in the new tab. (The link should ended with a image file extension .png .jpeg .jpg .gif etc... videos are not supported)

Convert any website to base16 color schemes with smooth transitions

Base16 Everything

Transform every website to match your color scheme.

Base16 Everything is a browser extension that remaps website colors to any base16/base24 (https://github.com/tinted-theming/home) palette in real time. Your browser finally matches your terminal, editor, and desktop.


## How it works

1. Install the extension
2. Pick a color scheme (or let it sync from your system)
3. Every website is instantly recolored to your palette

Color mapping is powered by Rust compiled to WebAssembly — it runs entirely in the browser with no external requests.

## Features

### Free tier — no account needed:

- Two built-in themes (Gruvbox Light Hard + Google Dark)
- Automatic light/dark mode switching based on system preference
- WASM-powered color mapping with smooth transitions
- Per-page activation toggle

### Premium ($8 one-time purchase):

- 200+ color schemes from tinted-theming/schemes (https://github.com/tinted-theming/schemes)
- Search and download any tinted-theming scheme directly from the popup
- Native config sync — read your palette from `~/.config/base16-everything/config.yaml`
- Lifetime updates, use on up to 3 devices

## System theme sync with tinty

Premium users can sync their browser theme with their system using tinty (https://github.com/tinted-theming/tinty). When you run `tinty apply`, your terminal, editor, desktop, and browser all switch together.

This works through tinted-web (https://github.com/mat-his/tinted-web), a tinted-theming adapter that ships 184 pre-built base24 themes.

### Setup

1. Clone the Base16 Everything's repository:

   ```bash
   git clone https://github.com/mat-his/base16_everything_home.git
   cd base16_everything_home
   ```

2. Install the native messaging host (native-host/) so the extension can read your local config:

   ```bash
   cd native-host
   ./install.sh <your-extension-id>
   ```

   To find your extension ID:
      1. Open chrome://extensions
      2. Find 'Base16 Everything'
      3. Copy the ID (looks like: mlmhenlobfodphglalpgjpinfidhcbio)

3. Add tinted-web to your tinty config (`~/.config/tinted-theming/tinty/config.toml`):

   ```toml
   [[items]]
   name = "base16-everything"
   path = "https://github.com/mat-his/tinted-web"
   themes-dir = "themes"
   supported-systems = ["base24"]
   hook = "cp $TINTY_THEME_FILE_PATH ~/.config/base16-everything/config.yaml"
   ```

4. Sync and apply:

   ```bash
   tinty install
   tinty apply base24-gruvbox-dark
   ```

The extension picks up the new palette on the next page load. No restart needed.

### Additional Tips

Don't forget to sync your system light/dark mode.
If this parameter is not configured correctly, you may notice glitches when switching themes — inconsistent colors, partial theme reloads, or web pages that don't match the rest of your environment.

Most modern Linux desktops rely on gsettings to define the preferred color scheme. On GNOME-based systems, toggling between light and dark modes can be done with a simple command:

```bash
gsettings set org.gnome.desktop.interface color-scheme prefer-light
# or
gsettings set org.gnome.desktop.interface color-scheme prefer-dark
```

This value acts as a global signal for GTK applications. When set correctly, GTK-based software automatically adapts its appearance to match the selected mode.

If you're building an automated workflow — where themes switch during the day or at night — this setting becomes essential. It ensures GTK applications respond consistently to theme changes.


## Account management

After purchasing a license, you can manage your account at https://base16everything.com/devices. Log in with your email to:

- View and revoke registered devices
- Reveal your license key

You can also access this from the extension popup via **Settings > Manage Devices**.

## Links

- Website & Pricing: https://base16everything.com
- tinted-web adapter: https://github.com/mat-his/tinted-web — tinty integration with 184 pre-built themes
- tinted-theming: https://github.com/tinted-theming/home — the base16/base24 ecosystem

## License

MIT LICENSE

THIS EXTENSION IS FOR BETA TESTING. An efficient ad blocker that blocks ads across the web.

uBlock Resurrected (BETA)

uBlock Resurrected is an experimental content blocker built from the ground up to bring advanced filtering capabilities to the Manifest V3 (MV3) engine. This is a BETA version intended for testing and development feedback.

Current Status & Beta Limitations:
Engine: Successfully ported core filtering logic to the MV3 declarativeNetRequest and scripting APIs.

UI Work-in-Progress: Users may encounter display issues in the Logger iframe and legacy links to original documentation pages.

YouTube Compatibility: Due to frequent changes in platform architecture, ad-blocking on YouTube may be intermittent as we refine our rulesets.

Known Conflicts: May conflict with other privacy extensions like Privacy Badger or Ghostery. For the best experience, we recommend testing with other blockers disabled.

Platform: Currently optimized for Chrome only.

Logging: Per-page block counters on the extension icon are currently disabled to optimize performance.

Roadmap for Stable Release:
Enhanced Tools: Consolidation of the element zapper and picker into a unified interface.

Advanced Logic: Implementation of keyword-based DOM filtering and procedural cosmetic rules.

Security: Improved firewall rules and network request interception.

Optimization: Full code refactoring to modern ECMAScript standards and UI/UX overhaul.
